basics.name: Tommy Hosmer
basics.label: PhD Student
basics.summary: Graduate student in computational engineering with experience in computational plasma physics and model order reduction.

work.name: University of California, Berkeley
work.summary: Advised by Professor Tarek Zohdi. Building physics-based simulations to test algorithms for path planning of autonomous vehicles in agriculture.

volunteer.summary: Remote tutor for K-12 students from underserved communities. Subjects tutored include mathematics and programming.

courses: Numerical Solutions to Partial Differential Equations

references.name: Professor Tarek Zohdi
references.reference: My advisor with expertise in computational mechanics and applied mathematics. Email: zohdi@berkeley.edu

projects.name: Senior Thesis: 'Digital Twin and Machine Learning Frameworks for Control of Data Center Energy Management Systems'
projects.summary: Developed artificial neural network for reduced order model of data center and physics based model of energy management system. Genetic algorithm was applied for optimizing power flow. Code developed in Python with the Tensorflow and Keras libraries.

projects.name: Low-Rank Approximation for Convolutional Layers in Super Resolution Applications
projects.summary: Implemented Singular Value Decomposition and Canonical Polyadic Decomposition for low-rank approximations of 4-D kernel tensors in the convolutional layers of the Enhanced Super Resolution Generative Adversarial Network. Inference times improved on the order of 1-3%. Report available upon request. Code developed in Python with the PyTorch and Tensorly libraries.

projects.name: Introduction to Artificial Neural Networks
projects.summary: Wrote a guide for beginning programmers on the fundamental theory and implementations of artificial neural networks. Developed a step-by-step guide in a Jupyter notebook with solutions. Published on AIFS website.
